CORPORATION after


C|TY'S POWER SITE

Surface Signs Show Plan to Deprive City OF
Last Location Available for Municipal


Light Plant

pages.raw_text:
Is the present agitation over the
source of Spokane's water supply
a scheme of private parties to get
control Of the dam site near the up
river pumping station, which Is
second in power only to the Wash
ing Water Power falls on Post st. ?


Two years ago at dinner given
by F. Lewis Clark, proposition
was made that private parties buy
the city water system and run 1t.
So much opposition developed as
soon as the project became known
that 1t was dropped. Since that
time there has been kept up at in
tervals agitation of a water supply
from some of the nearby lakes.
Should it be decided to obtain lake
supply, the up-river dam would be
for sale. This is the only place
left on the river where power may
be obtained. The falls at Trent,
Post Falls and Nine Mile are now
controlled by the Graves and
Washington Water Power Co. in
terests.


r. N. Smith, a civil engineer, who
has offered to make surveys to as
certain the feasibility of lake
supply, gratis, Is employed In the
Graves Offices. He is a salaried
man and oners to spend almost 1
year without pay for the benefit"
of the city.


Mr.. Smith was employed in the
city engineering department for
almost years and we'll knows that
that Office is in possession of data,
plans and estimates Of lake sup
ply which it will take year to
collect. He could easily secure
and go over this data to make his
report. Yet he offers to devote a
year of his time for no recompense.


When it is considered that no
other surveys are needed, the deal
has a queer aspect and appears on
its face to be scheme to secure
the city site.


There Is no question but that a
6water supply from a lake that
could be protected from contain
ination would be an ideal source for
the city to draw upon. and no good
and specific reasons why such a
lake could not be found and utilized
has yet been given.


The cost would be great, but the
supply would be worth the money.
At present number OF physicians
and other interests are talking
lightly of investing nearly a 81
100000 in the doubtful expedient OF
a filtration plant that. II capable of
